LAW AND MOTION 301, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION CONTINUED FROM SEP-09-2008 TO LAW AND MOTION AT SEP-24-2008, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 301 PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES.

LAW AND MOTION, 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER LABORERS' INTERNATIONAL UNION OF NORTH AMERICA'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS OFF CALENDAR AT THE REQUEST OF THE MOVING PARTY. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

PROOF OF SERVICE OF MEMO OF P&A IN OPPOS TO AMENDED PETITION AND MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION; DECLARATION OF RUTH M. BOND IN SUPPORT OF OPPOS TO MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION; DECLARATION OF MICHAEL DE BELLIS IN OPPOS TO MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION; DECLARATION OF MARY HAO IN SUPPORT OF OPPOS; REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF OPPOS; POS FILED BY RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO SERVED NOV-07-2008, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO PETITIONER LABORERS' INTERNATIONAL UNION OF NORTH AMERICA LOCAL UNION NO. 261

LAW AND MOTION 301, JUDGE BUSCH MADE A DISCLOSURE ABOUT HIS WIFE, CATHARINE BARNES, A DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY NOT INVOLVED IN THIS CASE AND THAT HE DOES NOT VIEW HER EMPLOYMENT AS A BASIS FOR RECUSAL. HE ALSO DISCLOSED ABOUT ELIZABETH SALVASON WHOSE NAME APPEARS ON THE PLEADING IS A LONG TIME FAMILY FRIEND AND FORMER PARTNER IN PRIVATE PRACTICE. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. PETITIONER LABORERS' INTERNATIONAL UNION OF NORTH AMERICA LOCAL UNION NO. 261'S MOTION FOR AMENDED PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. ARBITRATION IS DENIED AS TO TRAINING ISSUE FOR FAILURE TO EXHAUST. GRANTED AS TO THE TRANSFER DISCRIMINATION ISSUE WHICH IS AGREED AND AS TO THE CLAIM OF THE EXISTENCE OF A DISCRIMINATORY PRACTICE AGAINST UNION MEMBERS AS OPPOSED TO A DECISION TO HIRE PARTICULAR INDIVIDUAL WHO MAY HAVE BEEN HIRED. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. CONCHITA LOZANO-BATISTA OF THE LAW FIRM OF WEINBERG, ROGER & ROSENFELD, TEL. NO. (510) 337-1001 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER AND MOVING PARTY;
ATTY. RUTH BOND, ASSISTANT CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3976 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING -JUDGE BUSCH MADE A DISCLOSURE ABOUT HIS WIFE, CATHARINE BARNES, A DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY NOT INVOLVED IN THIS CASE AND THAT HE DOES NOT VIEW HER EMPLOYMENT AS A BASIS FOR RECUSAL. HE ALSO DISCLOSED ABOUT ELIZABETH SALVASON WHOSE NAME APPEARS ON THE PLEADING IS A LONG TIME FAMILY FRIEND AND FORMER PARTNER IN PRIVATE PRACTICE. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. PETITIONER LABORERS' INTERNATIONAL UNION OF NORTH AMERICA LOCAL UNION NO. 261 Motion For Amended Petition To Compel Arbitration - ARBITRATION IS DENIED AS TO TRAINING ISSUE FOR FAILURE TO EXHAUST. GRANTED AS TO THE TRANSFER DISCRIMINATION ISSUE WHICH IS AGREED AND AS TO THE CLAIM OF THE EXISTENCE OF A DISCRIMINATORY PRACTICE AGAINST UNION MEMBERS AS OPPOSED TO A DECISION TO HIRE PARTICULAR INDIVIDUAL WHO MAY HAVE BEEN HIRED. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =301/PJB/AY
